name: ScanTech AI Systems Inc.
business: ScanTech AI develops and deploys security screening systems using proprietary fixed-gantry Computed Tomography (CT) scanning technology that detects explosives, weapons, narcotics, and other contraband. Its initial market focus is domestic and international aviation checkpoints. The company also targets other government facilities and private sector venues.
sellsTo: government facilities (border crossings, seaports, military bases, embassies, federal buildings, prisons, postal facilities)
private sector (manufacturing plants, entertainment facilities, power plants, petrochemical facilities, convention centers, schools, sports stadiums, other public venues)

exposures.sentence: We have developed a proprietary fixed-gantry Computed Tomography (CT) scanning system that detects explosives, weapons, narcotics and other contraband.

We believe a significant market opportunity also exists for deploying our scanners in (i) other government facilities such as border crossings, seaports, military bases, embassies, federal buildings, prisons and postal facilities
ScanTech has developed, and is currently beta-testing, a proprietary set of AI-driven software modules to shorten the development path for these algorithms

catalysts.sentence: The Business Combination was consummated on January 2, 2025 (Closing).

catalysts.event: Issuance of additional shares to non-redeeming Mars shareholders
catalysts.sentence: On April 2, 2025, each Mars shareholder who elected not to redeem at the Closing and held their shares continuously since then was issued two additional shares of Common Stock.

The CRADA expires on November 5, 2025 and provides for collaboration between ScanTech and the TSL in developing, improving and optimizing the performance of the Sentinel to promote and strengthen transportation security.

tamClaims.claim: Global security screening market currently $7.6B, expected to reach $10.5B by 2029, growing at ~6.6% per year.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Mordor Intelligence in its Security Screening Market Size & Share Analysis Growth Trends & Forecasts (2024 2029) report, the global security screening market is currently estimated at approximately $7.6 billion and is expected to reach approximately $10.5 billion by 2029, growing at approximately 6.6% per year.

tamClaims.claim: Accessible aviation and infrastructure security screening markets for its checkpoint scanners over next 5 years is $17.8B ($8.5B aviation + $9.3B infrastructure), with ScanTech projecting 4.3% capture or $810M.
tamClaims.sentence: ScanTech management estimates that the accessible portion of the aviation and infrastructure security screening markets for its checkpoint scanners over the next 5-years is $17.8 billion (i.e., $8.5B aviation plus $9.3B infrastructure). Of the accessible market, ScanTech projects capturing 4.3% or just over $810 million of the $17.8 billion total accessible market for its scanners over the next five-year period.
